source.title: Structural Damage - Crane/Lifting Incident on Enterprise 205

event.top_event: Traveling block struck crown saver assembly
actions_taken: Drawworks clutch disengaged to stop upward movement
Traveling block slacked off approximately 10 feet
Root Cause Investigation conducted by contractor and operator
BSEE follow-up inspection performed on July 11, 2024
event.summary: On July 8, 2024, at 1908 hours, while pulling out of the hole with drill pipe on the jack-up Enterprise 205 at South Timbalier Block 23, the traveling block struck the crown saver assembly with approximately 117,000 lbs overpull, resulting in $11,210 in damages to the crown saver and crown-o-matic. The driller lost situational awareness by focusing on pipe tally while pulling, and the crown-o-matic failed to trip when contacted by the drill line because the barrel was not set properly. The incident was not immediately reported to BSEE.
recommendations: Develop rig-specific procedure for checking the crown-o-matic with step-by-step instructions
Ensure Job Safety Analysis identifies the hazard of traveling block striking crown block assembly
Implement requirement for crew member to monitor wire rope and count wraps during crown-o-matic check
Ensure proper setup and maintenance of crown-o-matic barrel
Improve communication of job instructions for pulling out of hole operations
Establish fleetwide procedure for checking crown-o-matic

hazards.name: Uncontrolled upward movement of traveling block during pull out of hole
Loss of situational awareness during drilling operations

threats.name: Traveling block continues upward past intended stop point
Crown-o-matic barrel improperly set, preventing trip mechanism activation
Driller diverts attention from pipe movement to pipe tally during pull out
Floorhand fails to count tool joints as drill pipe ascends

consequences.name: Traveling block strikes crown saver assembly
Structural damage to crown saver and crown block

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for pull out of hole task
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures for POOH operation

supporting_text: Although a JSA was used for pulling out of the hole, it did not state the hazard of the traveling block striking the crown block assembly

controls.name: Crown-o-matic (COM) device - mechanical trip mechanism
controls.barrier_role: Automatically stop traveling block when it reaches crown block
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical safety device to prevent overpull

supporting_text: the COM failed to trip when contacted by the wire rope because the barrel wasn't set properly
According to Cantium's report, the drill line contacted the COM, but it did not move the barrel enough to trip the COM

controls.name: Driller monitoring and control of drawworks during POOH
controls.barrier_role: Operator maintains awareness and control of pipe movement
controls.lod_basis: Operator situational awareness and manual control

supporting_text: DR1 lost situational awareness by checking the pipe tally while pulling out of the hole
While picking up to check the crown-o-matic and rack back stand, I looked at the pipe tally to count stands to top of liner and C.O.M. didn't trip
With the drawworks still engaged, the DR1 decided to focus his attention on the pipe tally

controls.name: Floorhand monitoring of ascending drill pipe and tool joint counting
controls.barrier_role: Crew member tracks pipe movement and alerts driller to position
controls.lod_basis: Crew communication and monitoring during POOH

supporting_text: FH1, who was wiping the ascending drill pipe, was not counting tool joints as the drill pipe was being pulled
Floorhand one (FH1) was wiping drilling fluid off the ascending drill pipe

controls.name: Rig-specific procedure for checking crown-o-matic
controls.barrier_role: Documented procedure ensures consistent and safe COM checking

supporting_text: When asked for a copy of the rig specific procedure for checking the (COM), the operator could not provide it
the Offshore Installation Manager OIM was asked for a rig specific procedure for checking the COM, he stated they did not have a step-by-step procedure for checking the COM

controls.name: Crew member posted to monitor wire rope and count drum wraps
controls.barrier_role: Dedicated observer ensures COM trip mechanism is properly activated
controls.lod_basis: Crew communication and monitoring during COM check

supporting_text: DR1 also failed to have a drill crew member watch the wire rope as he was checking the COM
post a crew member to count the wraps on the drum until the COM trips

controls.name: Proper setup and maintenance of crown-o-matic barrel
controls.barrier_role: Ensure COM barrel is correctly positioned to activate trip mechanism

supporting_text: the COM failed to trip when contacted by the wire rope because the barrel wasn't set properly

controls.name: Driller disengages drawworks clutch upon detecting overpull
controls.barrier_role: Stop upward movement of traveling block when overpull is detected
controls.lod_basis: Manual intervention to arrest block movement

supporting_text: DR1 heard a change in sound as the traveling block struck the crown saver assembly with 117,000 lbs overpull. He disengaged the drawworks clutch stopping the upward movement
Saw weight indicator jump up so I slacked off

controls.name: Immediate notification to BSEE of incident

supporting_text: The BSEE Houma District was not orally notified of this incident
The Operator failed to immediately notify The BSEE Houma District of the incident and it was first reported on July 9th, 2024, at 10:57AM

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
